content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

April 7, 2017

Tim Chapa
City Manager
City of Sanger
1700 7th Street
Sanger, CA 93657-2804

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-17-090

Dear Mr. Chapa:

This letter confirms receipt of your request for advice regarding your duties as
Sanger City Manager under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the "Act").¹

Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by
the requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor regarding
prospective conduct. (Section 83114(b).) After a careful review of the facts you provided,
we decline to provide written advice to you because your current question involves
analysis of conduct that has already occurred. Therefore, we decline your request for
advice since that would amount to advice on past conduct. (See, Regulation
18329(b)(8)(A).)

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.conclusion: Formal written advice is declined because the request involves analysis of conduct that has already occurred. The Commission declines to provide written advice since that would amount to advice on past conduct, which is not within the scope of prospective advice covered by Section 83114(b).

sections.question_synthetic: Does the City Manager of Sanger have conflicts of interest under the Political Reform Act regarding certain duties or conduct?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the requestor's question involved analysis of past conduct rather than prospective conduct, which falls outside the scope of formal written advice immunity under the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Declination letter (W-17-090). Document lacks explicit QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections but contains substantive analysis of why advice is declined. Reference to Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) supports declination basis. No verbatim question provided by requestor in this correspondence.

QUESTION: Does the City Manager of Sanger have conflicts of interest under the Political Reform Act regarding certain duties or conduct?

CONCLUSION: Formal written advice is declined because the request involves analysis of conduct that has already occurred. The Commission declines to provide written advice since that would amount to advice on past conduct, which is not within the scope of prospective advice covered by Section 83114(b).

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined a request for formal written advice from the City Manager of Sanger regarding conflict-of-interest duties under the Political Reform Act, citing that the question involved analysis of past conduct rather than prospective conduct.
